topic: Swiss C Permit After 5 Years or 10 Years: Why Settlement Timelines Differ by Nationality
angle: Explains why some applicants can reach a Swiss C permit after 5 years while others usually wait 10 years, with practical focus on route selection, nationality-sensitive timelines, and common misunderstandings.
notes_for_drafting: Do not collapse treaty-based, practice-based, and integration-based 5-year routes into one rule
Avoid implying that 5-year eligibility means automatic approval
Explain clearly that nationality can change the legal framework and evidence burden
used_title: Swiss C Permit After 5 Years or 10 Years: Why Settlement Timelines Differ by Nationality

topic: Do Student Years Count Toward a Swiss C Permit? How Residence Counting Really Works
angle: Clarifies how Swiss authorities assess residence history for C permit eligibility, with emphasis on student years, L permits, mixed permit histories, and timing mistakes before filing.
notes_for_drafting: Do not suggest that all lawful years in Switzerland count in the same way
Use mixed-status examples to avoid oversimplifying residence counting
Separate first-five-year and final-five-year counting issues carefully

topic: Can You Lose a Swiss C Permit by Living Abroad Too Long?
angle: Explains lapse risk, advance preservation requests, repeated short returns, and centre-of-life problems for C permit holders spending extended periods outside Switzerland.
notes_for_drafting: Do not describe the C permit as unconditional permanent residence
Distinguish card renewal from the underlying status clearly
Avoid over-focusing on short trips; explain centre-of-life and advance preservation issues
